title: Hadwiger's Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
statement_text: For every integer $t\geq 2$, if $G$ is a graph which does not contain $K_{t}$ as a minor, then $\chi(G)\leq t-1$.
context_text: Hadwiger's conjecture is described as perhaps the most important open problem in graph coloring. The entire paper is motivated by developing a hypergraph analogue of this conjecture, generalizing the notion of graph minors to hypergraphs and studying the resulting chromatic bounds.
paper_contribution: Proves a weak hypergraph extension: for every $t\geq 1$ there exists a finite integer $h(t)$ such that every hypergraph with no $K_t$-minor is $h(t)$-colorable, and establishes $\left\lceil\frac{3}{2}(t-1)\right\rceil\leq h(t)\leq 2g(t)$ where $g(t)$ is the maximum chromatic number of $K_t$-minor-free graphs.

title: Conjecture 2
statement_text: For every integer $t\geq 2$ we have $h(t)=\left\lceil\frac{3}{2}(t-1)\right\rceil$. In other words, if a hypergraph $H$ does not contain $K_{t}$ as a minor, then $\chi(H)\leq\left\lceil\frac{3}{2}(t-1)\right\rceil$.
context_text: The function $h(t)$ is the smallest integer such that every $K_t$-minor-free hypergraph is $h(t)$-colorable. The paper proves the lower bound $h(t)\geq\left\lceil\frac{3}{2}(t-1)\right\rceil$ by an explicit construction, and conjectures this lower bound is tight. The conjecture remains open even for $t=3$, where the best known upper bound is $4$.

title: Problem 1
context_text: Conjecture 2 predicts $h(3)=3$, but the best known upper bound for the chromatic number of $K_3$-minor-free hypergraphs is $4$ (from Corollary 2 of the paper). Problem 1 isolates the smallest open case of Conjecture 2.
